operating_state: Total operating revenues increased 23.3% to $2.66 billion driven by Telxius and CoreSite acquisitions contributing European, Latin American and data center assets. Europe segment revenue increased substantially from acquisitions while Latin America grew from expanded footprint and U.S. & Canada remained flat as colocations were offset by T-Mobile related churn. Services revenue doubled reflecting expanded tower-related services. Adjusted EBITDA grew 13% reflecting increased gross margin partially offset by higher SG&A expenses.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $1.94 billion from acquired assets and elevated fuel costs driving pass-through revenue increases across international segments. Depreciation, amortization and accretion increased 56% driven by intangible asset amortization from recent acquisitions. SG&A expenses rose 61% including bad debt expense in Asia-Pacific, personnel costs from acquisitions, and increased stock-based compensation including CoreSite-related equity awards.
material_events: The company repaid $962.9 million of CoreSite debt in January and $600 million of senior notes upon maturity, funded through credit facility borrowings and cash. In April 2022, AMT issued $1.3 billion in new senior notes comprising notes due 2027 and 2032. The Board elected Kelly C. Chambliss as director and declared quarterly distributions of $1.40 per share.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation provided positive $94.4 million comprehensive income impact though reduced revenue by approximately $26 million from weakness in Indian Rupee, Ghanaian Cedi, Nigerian Naira and Latin American currencies. The company holds EUR-denominated debt with 44% of revenues and 49% of operating expenses in foreign currencies, creating significant exchange rate exposure. Elevated fuel costs increased pass-through revenue and expenses across international segments.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued elevated churn in U.S. & Canada segment for several years due to T-Mobile master lease agreement terms including legacy Sprint lease cancellations. The company expects 2022 total capital expenditures of $2.0 to $2.1 billion including construction of approximately 6,000 to 7,000 communications sites globally. Management maintains remaining stock repurchase programs and continues evaluating equity funding sources for strategic initiatives.
uncertainty_profile: India tax assessment remains under appeal with management estimating successful defense probability and seller indemnification coverage. Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $99.2 million with potential changes over twelve months. The company maintains exposure to LIBOR transition with variable rate debt facilities and debt covenant compliance requirements including consolidated total leverage ratio and senior secured leverage ratio requirements.
